DispatchConditionRemarks.Para: May be dispatched with a FLAP ALTERNATE SWITCH fault provided:

ListItem.Para: Flap lever position transducers operate normally.
At least one surface (flap or slat) alternate control is verified to operate normally once each flight day.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Verify operation of slat and/or flap alternate control once each flight day (AMM DDG 27-51-08).

ListItem.Para: Confirm flaps are UP.
For all three hydraulic systems available, turn hydraulics on.
For only one or two of the hydraulic systems available at once:
With hydraulics off, move the speedbrake lever UP.
Pressurize and depressurize hydraulic systems in this sequence: left, right.
Pressurize the center hydraulic system.
Confirm that all spoilers are extended.
Select ALTN on the ALTN FLAPS ARM switch.

Note.Para: The FLAP/SLAT CONTROL caution message will be displayed.

ListItem.Para: Move the ALTN FLAPS selector to EXT.

Note.Para: If hydraulic systems are pressurized, spoilers, ailerons, and flaperons will move during test.
Advise the flight crew if a surface (slat or flap) alternate control is inoperative.

ListItem.Para: After movement of the appropriate surface is verified on expanded flap or slat position indications, move the ALTN FLAPS selector to OFF.
Wait at least 30 seconds.
Move the ALTN FLAPS selector to RET until leading and trailing edges are fully retracted.
Move the ALTN FLAPS selector to OFF.
Disarm the ALTN FLAPS ARM switch.
Confirm the FLAP/SLAT CONTROL caution message is not displayed.
Retract spoilers if extended:
With hydraulics off, move the speedbrake lever DN.
Pressurize and depressurize hydraulic systems in this sequence: left, right, center.
Confirm that all spoilers are retracted.

OperationProcedure.Para: In the event the FLAP/SLAT CONTROL caution message is displayed in flight, alternate mode operation will be required. Accomplish the associated non-normal checklist, but with the following approach configuration and speed:

ListItem.Para: If alternate slat control is inoperative:
Use flaps 20 and VREF 30 + 30 for landing.
If alternate flap control is inoperative:
Choose one:
Flap position is 5 or less:

Note.Para: Ensure slats are in midrange position using alternate mode as required. Use current flaps and VREF 30 + 40 for landing.

ListItem.Para: Flap position is between 5 and 20:

Note.Para: Use current flaps and VREF 30 + 20 for landing.

ListItem.Para: Flap position is 20 or greater:

Note.Para: Use current flaps and VREF 20 for landing.
